from pathlib import Path
from docx import Document
path = Path("/tmp/informe_l1_s21.docx")
doc = Document()
doc.add_heading("Informe sintético", 0)
doc.add_paragraph("n=10")
doc.save(path)
d2 = Document(path)

exercises.code:
from docx import Document
levels = [("Resumen", 1), ("Método", 2), ("Anexos", 1)]
doc = Document()
for text, level in levels:
    doc.add_heading(text, level=level)
styles = [p.style.name for p in doc.paragraphs if p.style.name.startswith("Heading")]
print(len(styles), "|", styles)

exercises.code:
from docx import Document
metrics = [("Ticket mediano", "28.0"), ("Reclamos", "—")]
doc = Document()
doc.add_heading("Auditoría", 1)
table = doc.add_table(rows=len(metrics), cols=2)
for i, (k, v) in enumerate(metrics):
    table.rows[i].cells[0].text = k
    table.rows[i].cells[1].text = v
cells = [[c.text for c in r.cells] for r in table.rows]
print(cells[0], "|", cells[1], True)

exercises.code:
from pathlib import Path
from pypdf import PdfReader
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
path = Path("/tmp/digital_l1_s21.pdf")
c = canvas.Canvas(str(path)); c.drawString(100, 750, "Ticket mediano 28"); c.save()
reader = PdfReader(str(path)); text = "".join(p.extract_text() or "" for p in reader.pages)
print("28" in text, len(text) > 0)

exercises.code:
from pathlib import Path
import fitz
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
pdf, png = Path("/tmp/render_l1.pdf"), Path("/tmp/render-p1_l1.png")
c = canvas.Canvas(str(pdf)); c.drawString(72, 720, "hola"); c.save()
doc = fitz.open(str(pdf)); page = doc[0]
page.get_pixmap().save(str(png))
print(png.exists(), png.stat().st_size > 0)

exercises.code:
from pathlib import Path
from PIL import Image, ImageDraw
from pypdf import PdfReader
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
from reportlab.lib.utils import ImageReader
png, pdf = Path("/tmp/scan_l1.png"), Path("/tmp/scan_l1.pdf")
img = Image.new("RGB", (200, 100), "white"); ImageDraw.Draw(img).text((10, 40), "OCR", fill="black"); img.save(png)
c = canvas.Canvas(str(pdf)); c.drawImage(ImageReader(str(png)), 0, 0, width=200, height=100); c.save()
reader = PdfReader(str(pdf)); n_chars = sum(len(p.extract_text() or "") for p in reader.pages)
print(True, True, "|", {"needs_ocr": n_chars == 0, "n_chars": n_chars})

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: Theory de reportes DOCX/PDF: la opción «Para reutilizar presentación y auditar métricas en Python» es la que el paquete defiende; las demás contradicen el contrato.
Como skeptic descarto distractores: en reportes DOCX/PDF, el slim_packet apunta a «OCR / tratamiento de imagen».
No me fío de memoria suelta; el selfCheck de reportes DOCX/PDF se ancla a «Mismas métricas clave en dashboard, Excel y documento» según la theory activa.
Releo outcomes de reportes DOCX/PDF: «Provenance, checklist visual y hallazgos trazables» es la respuesta coherente con el texto del paquete.
Gate mental: si no es «Marcar needs_ocr (o equivalente) y no fingir PDF d», rompe el modelo mental de reportes DOCX/PDF del iDo.

confusion_points: Revisé si el fixture de s21 asume libs instaladas (numpy/pandas/openpyxl).
Confirmé que no uso knowledge de S22+ para resolver S21.
